teachingPoint: IR-T617-1 — T617 FlowCanvasWriter MUST enforce the DRAFT→PUBLISHED one-way state transition. Once a flow is PUBLISHED, no updateDocument call is permitted on that flow record. BOLA guard verifies the requesting tenant owns the flow canvas (flow.tenantId === ALS.tenantId) at ORDER 1. FLOW_IMMUTABLE guard verifies status !== PUBLISHED at ORDER 2 before any write. Published flows are immutable audit artifacts — any modification is a compliance violation.
positiveExample: T617: BOLA at ORDER 1 → FLOW_IMMUTABLE guard (status !== PUBLISHED) at ORDER 2 → storeDocument or updateDocument at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 4 → enqueue(FlowCanvasUpdated) at ORDER 5. Published flow write attempt → FlowImmutableRejected emitted immediately at ORDER 2.
negativeExample: T617 allows updateDocument on PUBLISHED flows — operator can silently alter a deployed flow definition without a new publication. Or T617 skips BOLA — any tenant can overwrite another tenant's canvas. Or T617 checks FLOW_IMMUTABLE after writing — partial write to published flow corrupts state.
discriminatingConstraint: BOLA ORDER 1 before FLOW_IMMUTABLE. FLOW_IMMUTABLE ORDER 2 before any write. No updateDocument after PUBLISHED. Any reorder or missing guard = BUILD_FAILURE. CF-18-1.
keywords: BOLA ORDER 1 FLOW_IMMUTABLE ORDER 2 DRAFT PUBLISHED FlowCanvasUpdated FlowImmutableRejected T617 FlowCanvasWriter one-way state

teachingPoint: IR-T618-1 — T618 FlowPublicationOrchestrator MUST run DFS cycle detection on the user-authored graph (WHITE/GRAY/BLACK coloring) at ORDER 1 before any type compatibility check or publication write. A cycle in a flow definition creates an infinite execution loop. Type compatibility per-edge is validated at ORDER 2 — each edge's source output type must be compatible with the target input type per schema. OCC DRAFT→PUBLISHED state transition at ORDER 3 — optimistic concurrency prevents double-publication.
positiveExample: T618: DFS cycle detection (WHITE/GRAY/BLACK) at ORDER 1 → type compatibility per-edge at ORDER 2 → OCC updateDocument(status:PUBLISHED, version:+1) at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 4 → enqueue(FlowPublished) at ORDER 5. CycleDetected emitted on cycle. TypeMismatch emitted on edge incompatibility. OCC_CONFLICT → FlowPublicationConflict.
negativeExample: T618 publishes without cycle detection — infinite loop deployed to production. Or T618 checks type compatibility before cycles — cycle-containing graphs still emit type errors instead of cycle error. Or T618 uses plain storeDocument instead of OCC — double-publication race creates two PUBLISHED versions.
discriminatingConstraint: DFS cycle detection ORDER 1, type compatibility per-edge ORDER 2, OCC DRAFT→PUBLISHED ORDER 3. Cycle or type check after write, or plain storeDocument = BUILD_FAILURE. CF-18-2.
keywords: DFS cycle detection WHITE GRAY BLACK type compatibility per-edge OCC DRAFT PUBLISHED FlowPublished CycleDetected TypeMismatch T618 FlowPublicationOrchestrator

teachingPoint: IR-T619-1 — T619 NodeTypeRegistrar MUST use atomic dual-write with SETNX before both writes. SETNX(node-type-reg-lock:{nodeTypeId}) at ORDER 1 prevents concurrent duplicate registrations. Both writes (node type record + capability index entry) MUST succeed atomically — if the second write fails, redis.del(lockKey) must be called in the catch block to release the lock and prevent a hung lock state.
positiveExample: T619: SETNX(node-type-reg-lock:{nodeTypeId}) at ORDER 1 → storeDocument(nodeType) at ORDER 2 → storeDocument(capabilityIndex) at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(audit) at ORDER 4 → enqueue(NodeTypeRegistered) at ORDER 5. Catch: redis.del(lockKey) to release on failure. NodeTypeAlreadyExists on lock held.
negativeExample: T619 writes nodeType then capabilityIndex without SETNX — concurrent registrations create duplicate node types. Or T619 skips redis.del in catch — failed second write leaves lock held forever, blocking all future registrations of the same nodeTypeId. Or T619 writes capabilityIndex first — partial state if nodeType write fails.
discriminatingConstraint: SETNX at ORDER 1 before both writes. redis.del(lockKey) in catch block. nodeType write at ORDER 2 before capabilityIndex at ORDER 3. Missing SETNX or redis.del = BUILD_FAILURE. CF-18-3.
keywords: SETNX node-type-reg-lock atomic dual write redis.del catch lock release NodeTypeRegistered NodeTypeAlreadyExists T619 NodeTypeRegistrar

teachingPoint: IR-T620-1 — T620 CodeInjectionProcessor MUST acquire a version lock on the target node before injection at ORDER 1. A pre-write audit snapshot is stored at ORDER 2 — this serves as the rollback pointer. The audit is append-only (storeDocument only, never updateDocument) because it forms a NON-REPUDIATION trail. Version lock prevents concurrent injections from overwriting each other. If injection fails after the audit is stored, the audit record itself is the rollback evidence.
positiveExample: T620: version lock at ORDER 1 → pre-write audit snapshot (rollback pointer) storeDocument at ORDER 2 → inject code at ORDER 3 → storeDocument(injection result audit) at ORDER 4 → enqueue(CodeInjected) at ORDER 5. Append-only: never updateDocument on audit records. InjectionConflict on version lock failure.
negativeExample: T620 injects without version lock — two concurrent injections overwrite each other's code. Or T620 stores audit after injection — if injection fails, no audit record exists for rollback. Or T620 updates audit records — audit trail is no longer non-repudiable.
discriminatingConstraint: Version lock ORDER 1, pre-write audit (rollback pointer) ORDER 2 before injection, append-only audit. Missing version lock or post-injection audit or updateDocument on audit = BUILD_FAILURE. CF-18-4.
keywords: version lock pre-write audit rollback pointer append-only NON-REPUDIATION CodeInjected InjectionConflict T620 CodeInjectionProcessor

teachingPoint: IR-T617-2 — T617 FlowCanvasWriter uses BOLA + FLOW_IMMUTABLE as the two-guard gate. BOLA is the ownership verification (tenant owns the canvas). FLOW_IMMUTABLE is the state machine guard (canvas is not PUBLISHED). Together they form the idempotent write gate for visual canvas operations. Differs from CART-LOCK-SETNX-001 (FLOW-16) — no concurrency lock needed because OCC at T618 handles concurrent publication. Canvas writes are allowed concurrent — only publication is serialized.
positiveExample: T617: BOLA only checks ownership. FLOW_IMMUTABLE only checks published state. No SETNX needed for canvas writes — concurrent writes to different fields are allowed. Single-publisher OCC in T618 serializes the final publication step.
negativeExample: T617 adds SETNX to canvas writes — adds unnecessary contention; users cannot simultaneously edit different parts of the canvas. Or T617 skips FLOW_IMMUTABLE — clients can overwrite published flows. Or T617 checks FLOW_IMMUTABLE with an async re-read — TOCTOU window between check and write.
discriminatingConstraint: Two-guard pattern: BOLA (ownership) + FLOW_IMMUTABLE (state). No concurrency lock on canvas writes — that is T618's responsibility. DRAFT state allows concurrent writes. PUBLISHED state blocks all writes.
keywords: two-guard BOLA FLOW_IMMUTABLE canvas write DRAFT PUBLISHED T617 FlowCanvasWriter ownership state-machine

teachingPoint: IR-T620-2 — T620 CodeInjectionProcessor extends NON-REPUDIATION-AUDIT-001 from FLOW-16. Injection audit records are append-only — each injection event creates a new audit entry. No updateDocument on injection audit. Three audit phases (PRE_WRITE, COMPLETE, FAILED) create separate storeDocument records. This is critical for security review of AI-generated code injections — the pre-write audit exists even if injection fails, providing a complete audit trail.
positiveExample: T620: each injection audit entry is a new storeDocument record. Three phases: PRE_WRITE (before injection), COMPLETE (after success), FAILED (after failure). Append-only: no updateDocument. Each record is independent — phases are never merged or updated.
negativeExample: T620 updates audit records to fix injection errors — audit trail is corrupted. Or T620 omits the PRE_WRITE phase — if injection fails, no evidence of attempted injection. Or T620 combines all phases in a single record and uses updateDocument — one compromised record invalidates entire trail.
discriminatingConstraint: Append-only injection audit with three phases. storeDocument only — no updateDocument. PRE_WRITE phase stored BEFORE injection attempt (ORDER 2). Extends NON-REPUDIATION-AUDIT-001. Missing PRE_WRITE or updateDocument on audit = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: append-only three phases PRE_WRITE COMPLETE FAILED NON-REPUDIATION injection audit storeDocument T620 CodeInjectionProcessor
